what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for the Segmented component: declares a SegmentedOption interface ({value?, label}), a SegmentedProps interface extending div HTMLAttributes (omitting onChange) with options (array of option-objects or strings), value, and an onChange callback, and declares the Segmented function plus a default export.
observations: options accepts a heterogeneous array: Array<SegmentedOption | string>
onChange signature is (value: string, e: React.MouseEvent) => void, passing both the chosen value and the click event
Extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> with Omit on 'onChange' so the native handler doesn't clash with the custom one
Doc comment frames it as 'the audience toggle' for 2-3 options, with the active option lifting onto a surface
standouts: The doc comment ties the component to a specific product use ('the landing audience toggle') rather than a generic description
conventions: Follows the paired .d.ts/.jsx convention for typing untyped React.createElement components
Uses JSDoc block comments on the interface for editor surfacing
notes: Type contract for a 2-3 option segmented toggle; options can be strings or {value,label}, onChange returns the value plus the event.
questions: What does 'lifts onto a surface' mean concretely in the CSS that this type doesn't show?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares exactly the props the .jsx implements.
intention_signals: Gives the runtime-created (React.createElement) Segmented control a typed surface so consumers get autocomplete and type-checking despite the implementation using no JSX.
contribution: Contributes the public type signature of the Segmented audience-toggle control, including the string-or-object option shape and the (value, event) onChange contract.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript declaration for a Segmented control component with string-or-object options, a value prop, and a (value, event) onChange callback over a div.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a typed contract for the design-system's segmented audience-toggle so consumers get type-safe props.
